beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check weather and camera gear.
beats.reasoning: Wyatt wakes with full energy on a sunny Monday. He's organized and checks conditions for potential photography or outdoor time later. Builds routine before shift.

beats.activity: Drive to Interbay fire station for 24-hour shift.
beats.reasoning: Commute from Beacon Hill to Interbay is ~15–20 min drive. Wyatt arrives for his 24-hour rotation shift start.

beats.activity: Morning shift duties: equipment check, station training, light calls.
beats.reasoning: Start of 24-hour rotation; standard morning station work. Note: Light Rail closure 9am–3pm doesn't affect Interbay-based firefighter.

beats.activity: Lunch break; grab food near station, catch first half of USA vs Wales match on station TV.
beats.reasoning: USA vs Wales match airs at 11am; Wyatt is at-station and can watch with crew. Prefers close friends/crew over big crowds, so station watch fits his personality.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift: respond to calls, station duties, light downtime.
beats.reasoning: Continue 24-hour rotation. Afternoon calls and routine station work. Sunny day means potentially higher activity.

beats.activity: Quick errand: stop at REI to pick up a new dry bag for kayaking.
beats.reasoning: REI is very close to station (same neighborhood). Wyatt is organized and values his kayaking gear; sunny weather inspires him to prep for water time after shift.

beats.activity: Return to station, evening shift duties, dinner prep with crew.
beats.reasoning: Back to station for final hours of 24-hour shift. Crew meal and evening routine.

beats.activity: End of shift; drive home with new dry bag.
beats.reasoning: 24-hour shift ends. Wyatt drives home from Interbay (~20 min). Full energy and sunny evening still available.

beats.activity: Take new dry bag and film camera down to Lake Union for evening kayak and photography.
beats.location_name: Green Lake Boat Rentals / Lake Union (Wallingford, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Sunny early June evening (sunset ~21:30) is perfect for kayaking and film photography. Wyatt is easily distracted and values this kind of spontaneous, mood-driven activity. Golden hour light ideal for his camera work.

beats.activity: Paddle on Lake Union, shoot film photos from kayak, enjoy sunset.
beats.reasoning: Extended evening on water; combines two passions (kayaking and photography). Fiercely organized Wyatt has prepped gear. Wind-down activity before bed.

beats.activity: Return home, shower, organize camera gear and dry bag, wind down.
beats.reasoning: Back home from lake. Wyatt is organized; he'll clean and store gear. Light routine before sleep. Still within same calendar day.
Full night of rest after active day. Sleep bar spans overnight into 2026-06-02, ending well before 06:00 cutoff (ends at 06:30 to show clear overnight span).

diary: Started the day early with coffee and camera checks—the weather looked perfect, so I knew I'd want to get out after shift. The station was quiet this morning, which meant more time for equipment drills. Caught the tail end of the USA-Wales match on the station TV during lunch; the crew was into it, though I had to duck out for calls. Grabbed a new dry bag at REI on my way back—been needing one for a while. The real highlight came after I clocked out. Lake Union at golden hour with my film camera felt like exactly what I needed. The water was smooth, light was incredible, and for a couple hours it was just me, the kayak, and the lens. Got some shots I'm excited to develop. Showered off the lake water and organized my gear before bed. Days like this remind me why I love this city.
thought_process: The sunny weather and early World Cup kick-off had everyone energized at the station, which set a good mood for the day. I knew the Light Rail closure wouldn't affect me since I drive to Interbay anyway. The shift itself was standard—nothing dramatic, which I appreciated. That REI stop was planned, but I almost skipped it to head straight home. Glad I didn't. The real decision came at 3:30pm: I could've collapsed on the couch, but I grabbed the new dry bag and camera instead. Forty-three feels different than it did five years ago; I'm more intentional about capturing moments now. The lake session was pure therapy after being on-call all day. Sometimes the best part of a 24-hour rotation is remembering there's a whole evening waiting on the other side.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
